name: Meridian Capital
description: Early-stage VC fund ($350M AUM) focused on Series A/B in B2B SaaS, fintech, and developer infrastructure. Based in San Francisco. Currently evaluating Aria for a $2.5M Series A lead investment.

key_people.name: Jason Wu
key_people.notes: Lead partner on the Aria evaluation. Former VP Product at Lattice. Direct, numbers-oriented communication style. Focuses on unit economics and retention metrics.

key_people.name: Lisa Tran
key_people.notes: Supports Jason on deal evaluation. Ex-Goldman, Wharton MBA. Runs detailed financial models. Will likely handle diligence deep-dive on Aria's financials.

portfolio_companies_relevant.name: Canopy
portfolio_companies_relevant.relationship: Portfolio company — potential overlap with Aria in workflow automation. Recommended framing: complementary (Aria = communication layer, Canopy = workflow layer).

portfolio_companies_relevant.name: TrueNorth
portfolio_companies_relevant.relationship: Portfolio company — analytics platform. No overlap with Aria.

investment_committee.notes: If targeting Apr 24 IC, all materials needed by Apr 21. If May 8, more breathing room.

open_loops.description: Data room access requested by Jason Wu (Apr 11). Draft reply prepared but not yet sent.
Q1 revenue breakdown by segment requested by Jason Wu (Apr 11). Included in data room.
Confirm Meridian IC timeline — need to ask Jason when they plan to bring Aria to investment committee.

linked_sources.title: Re: Aria x Meridian Capital — Series A diligence next steps
Intro: Maya Patel / Aria <> Jason Wu / Meridian

notes: Meridian is the leading Series A candidate. Jason Wu is the decision-maker; Lisa Tran runs diligence. Key risk: Canopy overlap perception — use complementary framing. IC meets biweekly Thursdays; target Apr 24 IC for fastest path to term sheet. Warm intro came through Sam Torres (co-founder) who worked with Jason at Lattice.
